reviews.review_description: Game is unplayable due to stuttering, waiting for response and fix from developer, otherwise I’ll refund it.

Edit: After more play and coming back from Bulletstorm VR this game is about a 7 out of 10. It’s the loading screens before going into each room, the bullets don’t sound or travel like real bullets they’re more like little pellets, the environments are basic. But I will say the intense feeling this game gives you in combat is still top notch and the gameplay balance is well

If you like Drop Dead the Cabin you’ll like this. This game receives A LOT of hate that made me not want to buy it but I took a chance. It’s exactly what I thought it was originally. It’s not a AAA game but it’s a good game from a small studio and the atmosphere, sound, and gameplay feel is there. Not so much graphics but I could say that about most of the games on the quest store and most not AAA games. It’s a fun game for what it is especially if you enjoy the world it puts you in and I do enjoy the semi dark atmosphere because this game fits into a category of its own and it’s a solid 8 out of 10 I gave it 5 stars because I also see the developers are still working on it & supporting the title which is a plus and the reason I bought. I own a lot of games here and from just playing it I rank this among the top because I can see the replay value in it instantly.

developer_response.body:
Update: If it helps, the gunplay in The Foglands is supposed to be less "realistic". I recommend throwing your guns at the bad guys to see what happens!

Thanks for checking out the game and leaving it a review! The whole team really appreciates it!

reviews.review_description:
The Foglands should have never been released in this condition.

I really wanted to love the game but after trying to for almost five months I can’t even really enjoy it yet. There are too many serious technical problems. My initial enthusiasm has waned to the point where I’m giving up on it.

I fear that the developers, despite their best intentions, will never be able to get this game working properly. It’s had several updates and it’s still a mess.

The performance on the Quest 3 is abysmal. The frame rate is generally low and continually hitches and stutters. I don’t recall any other Quest game that runs this consistently choppy.

Basic user interactions like choosing an item from the vendor during a run are incredibly clunky.

The slow motion power up during combat turns your arm into a wobbly mess negating any advantage the slow motion may have given you.

The combat is very poor overall.  You can stand right in front of an enemy and swing a weapon at its head and most of the time nothing happens. Yet if you step back and throw the weapon at them it works. Firing weapons feels, and often is, ineffectual. The previously mentioned choppy frame rate generally gets worse during the boss fights to the point where it makes the combat very frustrating.

It’s a real shame.  There’s a lot of creative ideas in this game and I love the general ambiance, but the technology is very far away from being able to present these ideas properly. The unrealized potential makes the game even more disappointing.

developer_response.body: Jioxx has been following the game since launch, and we really appreciate their feedback. We also understand that this player prefers full body turning, which maybe impacting framerate. The Foglands has a pretty steep learning curve and is definitely a game that players get used to. It can be quite difficult to overcome the first hour so it doesn’t appeal to everyone. The players that do tend to really really love it. We will continue to work on that and hope you will take another look in the future :)

reviews.review_description:
I really like the concept of Fogland. Mixing together western and science fiction themes is a pretty good bonus for me as a Firefly Fan 🙂 As a Roguelike you will fail and fail and fail forward the game but that is really the core of the game. Every playthrough feels different and there is quite a bit of variance in every run.

There are a few points which need polishing e.g. the missing weapon feedback and a few minor clipping errors, but the devs seem to be really dedicated and I am pretty sure tjat a lot of those issues will be solved soon. 

If you enjoy a good roguelike Foglands really is worth a look, especially because of the unusual setting and cool game mechanics. I also really enjoys the high quality voice acting of the game.

This game is very different. Kind of reminds me of borderland a little people have very high expectations for certain games this year but I’ve seen worse games on the store get better ratings so I don’t understand what the hate is all about this game is a very enjoyable game if that’s what you’re looking for for people who are interested in itfor the rest of the people leaving nasty reviews and saying dumb stuff. Why don’t you make a game become a developer and then leave it a cute opinion other than that please shut up. And please take several seats.

How is it possible that Meta allows this game to be in the main store? I wouldn't even play it again if it was for free. It's bad in every single category! Graphics bad, AI bad, level design bad, performance bad, gameplay bad, handling bad and loading screens after every corner. This definitely needs to be removed from the main store, DO NOT BUY IT!

developer_response.body:
(UPDATE  - 12/19/2023): The latest version 1.02 patch significantly addresses key feedback received from players since launch. We are listening, and we hope you have a better experience with this version. Also, please look forward to continued enhancements coming in 2024!
-
Thank you for the feedback! I am passing on your feedback to the developers for our patch roadmap. We take your investment in us and our game very seriously, and I will let you know as soon as we have an update!

reviews.review_description:
Edit: a lot of hate for this game in other reviews! I can actually see myself putting a lot of time into this game and am adjusting my score from 4 to 5 stars due to it having such a low rating atm in the store!

Original review: love the atmosphere and music in this game, but this game is not optimized for standalone yet. I'm playing on a quest 3. First noticed the jitter against my first enemy in the tutorial 😬. I've lost a couple weapons into oblivion as well. I did though manage to beat the first boss without too much trouble despite the 10+ enemies on screen at one point, so the game still WORKS, just needs optimization. Headshots don't inflict more damage than body shots which is a huge shame imo, and you can't turn subtitles off which I also find a bit annoying. If all of these things were fixed it would be a five star game. At this stage deserves 3.5 stars for all the jitter, but I'm optimistic it can get patched soon 🤞🙏

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the review! We just released v1.02, a quality-of-life patch, for The Foglands that focuses on optimization and performance, along with a bunch of quality-of-life improvements and bug fixes. Feel free to let us know any other feedback you have and thank you for engaging with our game :)

reviews.review_description: First off, I am a little biased because of my love for western aesthetics with a little mysticism mixed in, but that aside the gameplay is pretty good. The guns I've experienced so far have been fun, but what I've really been falling in love with is the melee and "improvised ranged weaponry". You don't get many VR roguelikes that let you just up and punch an enemy (and usually when they do let you it does pitiful damage, so having a punch that actually feels like a punch and not like your just ticking the enemy is a nice change of pace) or pelt them with whatever bits and bobs you may have on hand.

I been playing this game for a bit on my Meta Quest 3 and have been having a good time. It’s scratching that roguelike itch for me and I like the full on voice acting and story. The vibes remind me of a Western meets Fallout vibe. 

If you’re a fan of roguelike games then I suggest checking this one out for sure. I thought at first it would be to freaky for me but it’s really not a scary game at all. The options also have plenty of VR options as well which is nice to see. Standing mode, sitting mode, different control options, etc. 

Looking at the reviews it seems the developers are listening to feedback so I confident that any bugs in the game will be squashed in due time.

I had placed this game on my wishlist pre-release last year until I started to read the reviews. I decided to give it a try now because of updates and I can see how passionate the dev team is with the game. I was going to try 45 minutes to start but ended up playing 3.5 hours straight. I got invested in the story and mission. Well Told, a very small, enthusiastic team, is continuously improving the game for a better experience. They’re not giving up! The Foglands is a roguelite game with permadeath and procedurally generated maps each time you respawn. You spend a lot of time exploring and you WILL die…..over and over again. When you respawn to the start of the game, you’ll discover and enter new maps. It’s always changing and evolving. That’s the point of the game and part of the fun and challenge! There are lots of systems and secrets to learn and places to explore. Be very patient and you’ll get hooked. So put your big girl/boy panties on and go discover the different aspects of this game with little assistance. You have to take the time to figure it out. There are so many hours to play so you’ll get your money’s worth. There are the occasional bugs, glitches, and jankiness but I’m able to put that aside because the game is so much fun and addictive. MissChiefVR

Well only god thing is story and dubbing. Game is full of glitches fight and shooting is not satysfying and roguelite aspect relly not fun. Graphics is not bad but ganeplay is awful.  Even with 40% discount i felt robbed.  (Refund)

My thoughts 
Pros - I really like the story and about 80% of the gameplay.  I love the atmosphere and even enjoy the music.  So far, I'm liking the characters, and this is a unique game.  I can see myself putting a lot of time into this game.
Cons - There definitely needs some patchwork.  It stutters and for some reason I ended up with more than two weapons.  Not sure if that is a bug, but if it is, I'm sure it will get fixed.  Maybe provide a map or some direction so that we don't feel like we are just wandering around.  It gets a little confusing down in those tunnels/hallways.  Just a little more direction, I mean don't give it away lol.  
So, those are my thoughts.  I do not regret playing it so far.  Hopefully they will get some patches going and fix the stuttering first. Maybe make the loading screen transitions a little smoother.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your review! We just released a quality of life patch to address the performance of the game. We plan on addressing some of your other feedback in future patches so stay tuned and thank you for your interest in The Foglands!

reviews.review_description:
Games like The Foglands are what gives VR a bad name. Broken and bare bones, boring and unfinished with trailers screen shots and marketing that boarders on outright false advertising. 

Bought it on sale for 50% off and it's still not worth, one of the absolute worst games on the platform if not the worst bar none.

1/5 (Developers slap yourself)

developer_response.body: We have noticed that there are quite a few players that don't realize that The Foglands is a narrative-driven roguelite with over 100 levels, randomized per run. The game has a fully voice acted multi-run narrative with a secret boss at the end of the narrative adventure. There are also 32 keys hidden in select levels that unlock a variety of hidden rooms and abilities that you can mix and match throughout the game. The game also supports a barfight style of action where gun-play, melee and throwing are all fluid and experimental. There is a lot to discover in The Foglands but we are sorry that you were not able to fully enjoy these features. We have included more tutorialization and  pause menu updates to make these easier to find in our most recent patch. Thanks for your feedback!

reviews.review_description:
the game is unplayable. Every action you do it makes the game stutter.
you shoot: stutter
you take an item: stutter
something explode: BIG STUTTER
it also stutter while you're walking, running and when you crouch.
High from the ground is totally messed up, you fly one meter above the ground and even customizing the high there's no way to stay on the ground. Is even impossible to crouch. To pass under an obstacle i have to use the button to crouch and also crouch phisically.
Graphics are very low, the bonfire is like a sprite from a 8bit old game.
It's hard to understand what to do exactly, but short story long, you have to run around collecting gears and stuffs to improve your equipment and repeat, again and again and again and what it makes me go crazy, every 30 steps you take, there's a loading, a black screen with a circle for about 10 seconds and then you can proceed.

Refund

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your feedback! We just released v1.02, a quality-of-life patch, that signficantly addresses the game's performance with stuttering. We are looking into enhancing other topics mentioned, so look out for future patches and we appreciate your interest in The Foglands!

reviews.review_description: Ok there have been some low scoring reviews but I am thinking this was before an update as I am not finding the issues and glitches!!  The controls are easy to master, the background sounds, including the music and visuals are awesome.  I really think a level map and base map would be a great addition as I do feel a bit lost and want to know where I am.  Take note you do need a good set of VR legs.   I did find 30 mins play time for me was comfortable before I felt I should bail out and continue later.  It is definitely a Roguelite type game so expect when you die to re loop, but you do learn and that helps in the next run - Enjoy !!!

reviews.review_title: Game is good don’t get discouraged getit
reviews.review_description: Other reviews don’t make justice to this game.  It does have some bugs that need to be addressed. Like when want to break instantly and it never breaks. It’s a fun game to play and entertaining.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review! We just released a quality of life patch that addresses the game performance and other improvements. Feel free to let us know if you have any questions? We appreciate your interest in The Foglands!

reviews.review_description:
Welcome to "The Fogland" a roguelite journey through the eyes of Jim, a "Runner", sent to venture inside the mysterious fog to scavenge for scraps and resources.

Right from moment the game starts, it tells a tale full of intriguing characters and mysteries that are gradually revealed to player after many attempts at navigating the fog.

The narrative is seeminly cut short with the death of Jim before your introduction to the Stranger.....who explains that Jim was almost dead but brought back for reasons unknown to him each time "he dies," owing to the fact that the game is very much rogue-lite in nature.
Many may be put off by the rougelite moniker; however, the fogland is very much a different beast to many other roguelites.Instead of using roguelite elements as a way to marry a paper-thin narrative to an extended running time.The Fogland uses genre tropes to gradually build up and reveal the true narrative over repeated runs. And through talking with a quirky set of characters.

The core loop of upgrading stats, upgrading weapons and expanding perk pools may be familiar to fans of rogue lites. But not many have an emphasis on the story outside of indie darling "Hades"
The game plays out in a series of random rooms. The majority contain enemies, sometimes mixed with turrents/traps and light platforming. With the variety gradually expanding as you venture deeper.You are rewarded for exploring the rooms with chests containing scrap that you can use to buy items and weapons during the run. Or you can even send it back to the camp to start the next run better equipped. 

If your really lucky you can find a "stranger case" where you can select 1 of 3 random perks.The can range from a simple 25% recoil reduction or bullet speed increase to more exotic perks like explosive bullets.Finding runner keys allows you to open a locker back in camp expanding the available pool of stranger cards for the following run. It's always fun trying to find a set of cards that synergize with each other.

Guns are another highlight in the game,with each one being pretty unique in some way.The default revolver, for example, requires you to press the "B" button to empty the chamber before loading the ammo and flicking your wrist up to reload.But, buy the machinegun pistol and you may or may not realise you can load multiple clips at once.Every gun is also upgradeable back in the base if you manage to beat a boss and acquire "Ichor"Or you can choose to use the Ichor to upgrade your attributes of Jim giving you better survivability on the next run.

The game really rewards those who are prepared to go looking for it, and is full of little mysteries and hidden depths....even outside the story.There's slow-down mechanic, but I currently don't know how it's triggered.There's another system called "affinities" that I have no idea how it works......but I have an idea.

However, the game is not perfect; there are some small glitches. Eg some the enemy AI can be iffy sometimes....Ive occasionally had enemies just stand there, unflinching as I kill them from a distance.Luckly they don't happen often enough to really hamper my enjoyment.And also the corridors designed to mask loading times (?) still actually having a short 2-3 secs loading screen (though this may just be due to the limitations of a standalone device).

I must admit that I was a bit hesitant at picking up the title due to the mixed reviews at launch.
But I am glad that the devs have really taken the time to address the launch criticisms.

A good roguelite shooter which get even more interesting as you unlock more exotic perks. But when coupled with the intreguimg and mysterious story (so many questions from the opening alone!) it really elevates the game to something really special.

A special mention also goes to the unsung hero of audio and sound design. It really sets the tone of the world in a very subtle manner.....I really like how the layers build up with the action. Id really like to give the game 4.5 (just need a fw little glitches ironing out) But with no option for 1/2 mark I've given it a 5. I'm certain that the dev's are busy working on that final polish, making it a well deserved 5.

Really enjoy this game!
A roguelite cross between a western and sci-fi.
Graphics are very good, sound great, atmosphere also great!

Has a standing and seated mode.

Can play normal mode or story.. story is the easier version that concentrates on the story more than gameplay.

You unlock cards during play, that are used to upgrade your character and skills.
You also find keys they permanently unlock skills via lockers in the main hub.
During play, you find scrap metal that is used to purchase weapons during gameplay, or you can send the scrap metal back to the hub, and use them to purchase and upgrade when you return to the hub between dying and then going on your next run.

The game also has very good voice acting.
I have heard that the guns are getting controller feedback in an upcoming update too :)

A very fun and enjoyable single player rogue lite, with an interesting theme… some jump scares too lol

I recommend this as a worthy addition to your gaming library.

Thank you

reviews.review_title: I will give 5 stars if these get fixed.
reviews.review_description:
the game was not polished when released. This is a common theme amongst game developers. Rush it out to meet deadlines and patch later. I’m very sick of this practice in gaming. the enemy A.I. is terrible the attacks feel unsatisfying when shooting or punching. environmental interactions are terrible. There is lag when punching through wooden barriers and grabbing things. AND THE BULLETS WHEN FIRING THE GUN IS LITTLE ORANGE BALLS??? fix this…. When firing the gun just put flames and smoke at the end of the barrel and make the enemies react to where you shoot them. THE OVERALL LAG IS UNBELIEVABLE. did yall even test this game on a psvr2, quest2, quest3, quest pro? before pumping this out? A major patch is in high demand at this point. It just came out and people laid their money on the counter…

Edited: Also make it to where you pick up the loot individually not walk over it and it gravitate to you. It just looks cheap and the fire looks terrible as well. and I also experienced slow movement and had to press down on the left thumbstick to continue to move fast.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your detailed feedback. We just released a quality of life patch that improves performance on Quest 3 and Quest 2, as well as increases the speed of the bullet projectiles and other improvements. You can now set your sprint to toggle with one press of the thumbstick, for example. Feel free to let us know if you have any questions and we really appreciate your interest in The Foglands!

reviews.review_title: It's the concept of a game
reviews.review_description:
The idea is pretty nice but I would not recommend getting this game, here's why: 

- There's a few rules in VR game development one is to never move the player without him moving in physical space, this games controls leave you with no other choice but to accept being moved when you press the joystick as well as being moved upwards when you press a to jump. Both already make you quite dizzy but then this game includes yet another way to move you when your head collides with an object instead of just clipping inside the object the player is pushed out of that object.

>> This is how the developer could improve the game: Offer players the choice to teleport instead of pushing the camera with the joystick (the typical implementation where you press the joystick then aim a cycle on the ground and release to teleport there - way way less nauseating and you can still count that as movement in the game for the buffs) - just let people stick their head into objects... Yeah I know it's a bit ugly but it's still way better that having a collider on the camera and just being pushed around when you accidentally come on contact with a box. The jumping so far was not really crucial so I assume it's probably just for like locking off areas of the map or so and that would also be solved with the teleporting 

- The story is missing entirely. I bought the game then started a new round and so far I have no idea what's going on and I already faced the first boss. What annoys me the most about this is that the game asked what mode I want to play "challenging where it's focused on game play apparently" or "story where it should be less challenging but more story telling" and I chose story... Yet all I got was the tutorial where some unknown Marion talked to me then died then I was eaten by a worm and ended up in some cathedral looking train department shaking hands with some weird figure and then "woke up" in that same train department with the same figure who barely spoke to me. Then I kinda had to go to the lobbyish thing where there was some woman who apparently knew me and I knew her well apparently... She was kinda but not really shocked the other guy was eaten and then it was like yo let's go and you leave for a "run" and apparently it's super normal that when you die you just come back to her and apparently they all know and find it super normal or something I don't know... Nothing gets explained to you you don't know where you are or what you are trying to do, why there are zombies everywhere or who even died at the beginning. Why is there a strange man that can apparently make you immortal, why are there tarot card like things that give you powers - which btw you don't buy from the guy but buy in a far way room by just opening some lockers like... What the heck is all this?There is zero world building or story telling it's just try to survive as long as you can and then try again 

>> I'm not gonna list specifically what the dev could do better here as that should be kinda obvious but to say the least ease players into your world and into what ever story there might be or - if there's no story that's fine but then don't make up mystical creatures and tarot cards and all that just create a simple generic roage like game and ui that's selve explanatory and run with it 


To summarize: I'm disappointed this game has potential but it's not really well executed and for the price they ask when it's not on sale this game in my opinion is not worth it. I hope they improve the game and it will become something great but at its current state I don't see me enjoying this very long

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for playing our game and for the feedback! to answer your points:

- The movement type being just translation is definitely hard for some players initially, but The Foglands is a barfight and platforming style game. There are lots of areas to jump up to or down to and lots of corners to explore. I will definitely let the devs know your feedback though!

- The Foglands is actually a "story-driven" roguelite. The story of the game unfolds as you come upon the second boss, find more runner keys and unlock runner lockers and talk to all the characters. It is a multi run story but we totally understand that isnt immediately clear. Once you defeat the second boss, you will notice that the story objective in the pause menu will change and help to guide you through the rest of the story. 

- This is very good feedback, we over emphasized "mystery" which meant that there are players that don't immediately understand how to discover the game. Please feel free to check out this thread of tips ( https://x.com/WellToldEnt/status/1797707506015441168 )or join our discord server ( https://discord.gg/zBTCDSrv ) to chat with other players and hopefully help you get more out of the game :)
